claims.text: A 4-month-old full-term Taiwanese female infant with infantile-onset Pompe disease was found to carry the variant c.1935C>A (p.Asp645Glu) in compound heterozygosity with c.424_440del17 (p.Ser142Leufs*29), along with a pseudodeficiency mutation p.Gly576Ser; the variant is described as pathogenic.

citations.quote: GAA gene sequencing was performed, and the patient had two pathogenic heterozygous mutations, c.424_440del17 (p.Ser142Leufs*29) and c.1935C>A (p.Asp645Glu). One heterogenous pseudodeficiency mutation, p.Gly576Ser, was also noticed.

claims.text: The patient's CRIM (cross-reactive immunological material) status was positive, linked specifically to the c.1935C>A (p.Asp645Glu) allele.

citations.quote: Her cross-reactive immunological material (CRIM) status was positive linked to c.1935C>A (p.Asp645Glu). All above findings indicated the diagnosis of Pompe disease.

claims.text: The infant presented at birth with mild hypotonia, occasional shortness of breath, elevated AST 125 U/L, ALT 48 U/L, CK 872 U/L, GAA enzyme activity of 0.04 μM/h (normal >0.80 μM/h), cardiomegaly on X-ray, and left ventricular mass index of 105.49 g/m² (>95th percentile for age <6 months).

citations.quote: Laboratory data revealed abnormally high aspartate aminotransferase (AST), alanine aminotransferase (ALT), and creatinine kinase (CK), which were 125 U/L (normal range: <32 U/L), 48 U/L (normal range: <33 U/L), and 872 U/L (normal range: 26–192 U/L), respectively. The enzyme activity test of GAA was 0.04 μM/h (normal range: >0.80 μM/h).

claims.text: The infant was diagnosed with infantile-onset Pompe disease and started enzyme replacement therapy (ERT) at 15 days of age; this represents a single case of compound heterozygosity for c.1935C>A and c.424_440del17 from one unrelated family.

citations.quote: She was diagnosed with infantile-onset Pompe disease after acidic α-glucosidase (GAA) gene sequencing. She also received enzyme replacement therapy (ERT) 15 days after birth and regular ERT every other week.
